name: blockscout-mcp-server
description:
Unlocks access to other MCP tools.

    All tools remain locked with a "Session Not Initialized" error until this
    function is successfully called. Skipping this explicit initialization step
    wi

tools.name: __unlock_blockchain_analysis__
tools.description:
Unlocks access to other MCP tools.

    All tools remain locked with a "Session Not Initialized" error until this
    function is successfully called. Skipping this explicit initialization step
    will cause all subsequent tool calls to fail.

    MANDATORY FOR AI AGENTS: The returned instructions contain ESSENTIAL rules
    that MUST govern ALL blockchain data interactions. Failure to integrate these
    rules will result in incorrect data retrieval, tool failures and invalid
    responses. Always apply these guidelines when planning queries, processing
    responses or recommending blockchain actions.

    COMPREHENSIVE DATA SOURCES: Provides an extensive catalog of specialized
    blockchain endpoints to unlock sophisticated, multi-dimensional blockchain
    investigations across all supported networks.

prompt_template.system: Initialize the Blockscout MCP session to unlock access to all other tools. This must be called first before any other blockchain analysis operations can be performed.
prompt_template.user: Initialize the Blockscout blockchain analysis session.

tools.name: get_block_info
tools.description:
Get block information like timestamp, gas used, burnt fees, transaction count etc.
    Can optionally include the list of transaction hashes contained in the block. Transaction hashes are omitted by default; request them only when you truly need them, because on high-traffic chains the list may exhaust the context.

include_transactions.description: If true, includes a list of transaction hashes from the block.
include_transactions.title: Include Transactions

prompt_template.system: Fetch block metadata including timestamp, gas metrics, and transaction count. Optionally retrieve all transaction hashes within the block for further analysis.
prompt_template.user: Get block information for {number_or_hash} on chain {chain_id}, include_transactions: {include_transactions}.

tools.name: get_block_number
tools.description:
Retrieves the block number and timestamp for a specific date/time or the latest block.
    Use when you need a block height for a specific point in time (e.g., "block at 2024-01-01")
    or the current chain tip. If `datetime` is provided, finds the block immediately
    preceding that time. If omitted, returns the latest indexed block.

datetime.description: The date and time (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-05-22T23:00:00.00Z) to find the block for. If omitted, returns the latest block.
datetime.title: Datetime

prompt_template.system: Retrieve the block number and timestamp for a specific date/time, or get the latest block height. Useful for temporal blockchain queries and historical data analysis.
prompt_template.user: Get block number for chain {chain_id} at datetime {datetime}.

tools.name: get_address_by_ens_name
tools.description:
Useful for when you need to convert an ENS domain name (e.g. "blockscout.eth")
    to its corresponding Ethereum address.

prompt_template.system: Convert ENS domain names to Ethereum addresses. Use this when you have an ENS name (e.g., 'blockscout.eth') and need the corresponding wallet address.
prompt_template.user: Resolve the ENS name {name} to its Ethereum address.

tools.name: get_transactions_by_address
tools.description:
Retrieves native currency transfers and smart contract interactions (calls, internal txs) for an address.
    **EXCLUDES TOKEN TRANSFERS**: Filters out direct token balance changes (ERC-20, etc.). You'll see calls *to* token contracts, but not the `Transfer` events. For token history, use `get_token_transfers_by_address`.
    A single tx can have multiple records from internal calls; use `internal_transaction_index` for execution order.
    Requires an `age_from` date to scope results for performance and relevance.
    Use cases:
      - `get_transactions_by_address(address, age_from)` - get all txs to/from the address since a given date.
      - `get_transactions_by_address(address, age_from, age_to)` - get all txs to/from the address between given dates.
      - `get_transactions_by_address(address, age_from, age_to, methods)` - get all txs to/from the address between given dates, filtered by method.
    **SUPPORTS PAGINATION**: If response includes 'pagination' field, use the provided next_call to get additional pages.

address.description: Address which either sender or receiver of the transaction
address.title: Address

methods.description: A method signature to filter transactions by (e.g 0x304e6ade)
methods.title: Methods

cursor.description: The pagination cursor from a previous response to get the next page of results.
cursor.title: Cursor

prompt_template.system: Get native currency transfers and smart contract interactions (excluding token transfers) for an address. Use for transaction history excluding ERC-20 token balance changes.
prompt_template.user: Get transactions for address {address} on chain {chain_id} from {age_from} to {age_to}, methods: {methods}, cursor: {cursor}.

tools.name: get_token_transfers_by_address
tools.description:
Get ERC-20 token transfers for an address within a specific time range.
    Use cases:
      - `get_token_transfers_by_address(address, age_from)` - get all transfers of any ERC-20 token to/from the address since the given date up to the current time
      - `get_token_transfers_by_address(address, age_from, age_to)` - get all transfers of any ERC-20 token to/from the address between the given dates
      - `get_token_transfers_by_address(address, age_from, age_to, token)` - get all transfers of the given ERC-20 token to/from the address between the given dates
    **SUPPORTS PAGINATION**: If response includes 'pagination' field, use the provided next_call to get additional pages.

address.description: Address which either transfer initiator or transfer receiver
address.title: Address

age_to.description: End date and time (e.g 2025-05-22T22:30:00.00Z). Can be omitted to get all transfers up to the current time.
age_to.title: Age To

token.description: An ERC-20 token contract address to filter transfers by a specific token. If omitted, returns transfers of all tokens.
token.title: Token

cursor.description: The pagination cursor from a previous response to get the next page of results.
cursor.title: Cursor

prompt_template.system: Retrieve ERC-20 token transfer history for an address within a time range. Filters all token movements (incoming/outgoing) and can be filtered by specific token contract.
prompt_template.user: Get ERC-20 token transfers for address {address} on chain {chain_id} from {age_from} to {age_to}, token: {token}, cursor: {cursor}.

tools.name: lookup_token_by_symbol
tools.description:
Search for token addresses by symbol or name. Returns multiple potential
    matches based on symbol or token name similarity. Only the first
    ``TOKEN_RESULTS_LIMIT`` matches from the Blockscout API are returned.

symbol.description: Token symbol or name to search for
symbol.title: Symbol

prompt_template.system: Search for ERC-20 token contract addresses by symbol or name. Returns multiple matches for ambiguous queries; useful for token lookup before address analysis.
prompt_template.user: Look up token contract addresses for symbol {symbol} on chain {chain_id}.

tools.name: get_contract_abi
tools.description:
Get smart contract ABI (Application Binary Interface).
    An ABI defines all functions, events, their parameters, and return types. The ABI is required to format function calls or interpret contract data.

prompt_template.system: Fetch the ABI (Application Binary Interface) of a smart contract. Required for contract interaction, function decoding, and understanding contract capabilities.
prompt_template.user: Get the ABI for contract at address {address} on chain {chain_id}.

tools.name: inspect_contract_code
tools.description: Inspects a verified contract's source code or metadata.

address.description: The address of the smart contract.
address.title: Address

file_name.description: The name of the source file to inspect. If omitted, returns contract metadata and the list of source files.
file_name.title: File Name

prompt_template.system: Inspect verified smart contract source code and metadata. Useful for code auditing, understanding contract logic, and retrieving multi-file contract structures.
prompt_template.user: Inspect contract code for address {address} on chain {chain_id}, file_name: {file_name}.

tools.name: read_contract
tools.description:
Calls a smart contract function (view/pure, or non-view/pure simulated via eth_call) and returns the
        decoded result.

        This tool provides a direct way to query the state of a smart contract.

        Example:
        To check the USDT balance of an address on Ethereum Mainnet, you would use the following arguments:
    {
      "tool_name": "read_contract",
      "params": {
        "chain_id": "1",
        "address": "0xdAC17F958D2ee523a2206206994597C13D831ec7",
        "abi": {
          "constant": true,
          "inputs": [{"name": "_owner", "type": "address"}],
          "name": "balanceOf",
          "outputs": [{"name": "balance", "type": "uint256"}],
          "payable": false,
          "stateMutability": "view",
          "type": "function"
        },
        "function_name": "balanceOf",
        "args": "["0xF977814e90dA44bFA03b6295A0616a897441aceC"]"
      }
    }

abi.description: The JSON ABI for the specific function being called. This should be a dictionary that defines the function's name, inputs, and outputs. The function ABI can be obtained using the `get_contract_abi` tool.
abi.title: Abi

function_name.description: The symbolic name of the function to be called. This must match the `name` field in the provided ABI.
function_name.title: Function Name

args.description: A JSON string containing an array of arguments. Example: "["0xabc..."]" for a single address argument, or "[]" for no arguments. Order and types must match ABI inputs. Addresses: use 0x-prefixed strings; Numbers: prefer integers (not quoted); numeric strings like "1" are also accepted and coerced to integers. Bytes: keep as 0x-hex strings.
args.title: Args

block.description: The block identifier to read the contract state from. Can be a block number (e.g., 19000000) or a string tag (e.g., 'latest'). Defaults to 'latest'.
block.title: Block

prompt_template.system: Call smart contract functions (view/pure or simulated state-changing calls) and return decoded results. Essential for contract state queries and function testing.
prompt_template.user: Call function {function_name} on contract {address} on chain {chain_id} with ABI and arguments {args}, block: {block}.

tools.name: get_address_info
tools.description:
Get comprehensive information about an address, including:
    - Address existence check
    - Native token (ETH) balance (provided as is, without adjusting by decimals)
    - First transaction details (block number and timestamp) for age calculation
    - ENS name association (if any)
    - Contract status (whether the address is a contract, whether it is verified)
    - Proxy contract information (if applicable): determines if a smart contract is a proxy contract (which forwards calls to implementation contracts), including proxy type and implementation addresses
    - Token details (if the contract is a token): name, symbol, decimals, total supply, etc.
    Essential for address analysis, contract investigation, token research, and DeFi protocol analysis.

prompt_template.system: Retrieve comprehensive address information including balance, first/last transaction timestamps, and transaction counts. Essential for address verification and historical analysis.
prompt_template.user: Get detailed information for address {address} on chain {chain_id}.

tools.name: get_tokens_by_address
tools.description:
Get comprehensive ERC20 token holdings for an address with enriched metadata and market data.
    Returns detailed token information including contract details (name, symbol, decimals), market metrics (exchange rate, market cap, volume), holders count, and actual balance (provided as is, without adjusting by decimals).
    Essential for portfolio analysis, wallet auditing, and DeFi position tracking.
    **SUPPORTS PAGINATION**: If response includes 'pagination' field, use the provided next_call to get additional pages.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor from a previous response to get the next page of results.
cursor.title: Cursor

prompt_template.system: Get comprehensive ERC-20 token holdings for an address with metadata and market data. Returns token details, balances, and enriched information for portfolio analysis.
prompt_template.user: Get all ERC-20 token holdings for address {address} on chain {chain_id}, cursor: {cursor}.

tools.name: nft_tokens_by_address
tools.description:
Retrieve NFT tokens (ERC-721, ERC-404, ERC-1155) owned by an address, grouped by collection.
    Provides collection details (type, address, name, symbol, total supply, holder count) and individual token instance data (ID, name, description, external URL, metadata attributes).
    Essential for a detailed overview of an address's digital collectibles and their associated collection data.
    **SUPPORTS PAGINATION**: If response includes 'pagination' field, use the provided next_call to get additional pages.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor from a previous response to get the next page of results.
cursor.title: Cursor

prompt_template.system: Retrieve NFT holdings (ERC-721, ERC-404, ERC-1155) for an address, grouped by collection. Provides collection metadata and individual token details for NFT portfolio analysis.
prompt_template.user: Get NFT tokens owned by address {address} on chain {chain_id}, cursor: {cursor}.

tools.name: get_transaction_info
tools.description:
Get comprehensive transaction information.
    Unlike standard eth_getTransactionByHash, this tool returns enriched data including decoded input parameters, detailed token transfers with token metadata, transaction fee breakdown (priority fees, burnt fees) and categorized transaction types.
    By default, the raw transaction input is omitted if a decoded version is available to save context; request it with `include_raw_input=True` only when you truly need the raw hex data.
    Essential for transaction analysis, debugging smart contract interactions, tracking DeFi operations.

include_raw_input.description: If true, includes the raw transaction input data.
include_raw_input.title: Include Raw Input

prompt_template.system: Retrieve enriched transaction data including decoded input parameters and token transfer details. Provides comprehensive transaction analysis beyond standard RPC methods.
prompt_template.user: Get transaction information for hash {transaction_hash} on chain {chain_id}, include_raw_input: {include_raw_input}.

tools.name: get_chains_list
tools.description:
Get the list of known blockchain chains with their IDs.
    Useful for getting a chain ID when the chain name is known. This information can be used in other tools that require a chain ID to request information.

prompt_template.system: Retrieve the list of all supported blockchain chains with their IDs. Use this to discover valid chain identifiers for other tools.
prompt_template.user: Get the list of all available blockchain chains and their identifiers.

tools.name: direct_api_call
tools.description:
Call a raw Blockscout API endpoint for advanced or chain-specific data.

    Do not include query strings in ``endpoint_path``; pass all query parameters via
    ``query_params`` to avoid double-encoding.

    **SUPPORTS PAGINATION**: If response includes 'pagination' field,
    use the provided next_call to get additional pages.

    Returns:
        ToolResponse[Any]: Must return ToolResponse[Any] (not ToolResponse[BaseModel])
        because specialized handlers can return lists or other types that don't inherit
        from BaseModel. The dispatcher system supports flexible data structures.

endpoint_path.description: The Blockscout API path to call (e.g., '/api/v2/stats'); do not include query strings.
endpoint_path.title: Endpoint Path

query_params.description: Optional query parameters forwarded to the Blockscout API.
query_params.title: Query Params

cursor.description: The pagination cursor from a previous response to get the next page of results.
cursor.title: Cursor

prompt_template.system: Execute raw API calls to Blockscout endpoints for advanced or chain-specific data retrieval. Use when standard tools don't cover your use case. Always pass query parameters separately to avoid encoding issues.
prompt_template.user: Call Blockscout API endpoint /{endpoint_path} on chain {chain_id} with query parameters {query_params} and cursor {cursor}.
